programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and the team are called in to look for the body of a murdered Muslim man that has gone missing from the morgue. As they try to establish a link between a top-secret residue found on the corpse and the victim's killers, their investigations uncover an experimental Navy tracking system and a plot to sell nuclear material to terrorists planning to make a bomb. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd and Christabel Fellowes

Medical drama, starring Gray O'Brien and Simon Shepherd

programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

Frances Barber, Kenneth Cranham and Dave Hill guest star

Festive special from 1991, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.desc: In a festive episode first broadcast in 1995, Hyacinth resorts to alternative methods of recruitment when she gets no response to the adverts for a pageant she is directing. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When it emerges that the vessel is carrying an explosive payload, the team fears the launch of the newest addition to the Royal Navy's fleet is the target. Beth's arrival at Section D brings new information and feelings of familiarity to Harry, who is forced to put aside his concerns about the source and take drastic action. Sophia Myles (Moonlight) joins the cast, with Peter Firth and Richard Armitage

programme.title: Holby City

Jodie feels pushed aside when Sacha's daughter starts asking questions about his surprise engagement, but refuses to give up without a fight. Nicky does not know who to believe when Chloe accuses Cameron of abusing his position

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Finals week gets underway with the last Invention Test of the year. The six remaining contestants are divided into two teams, one of which prepares rabbit in mustard sauce, while the other makes bouillabaisse with a rouille and a crouton. The chef who presents the finest dish in each of the groups will be rewarded with a pass straight through to the final four, while the others must make a classic dessert to avoid elimination

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: News of a terrible event reaches the US base in Chioggia, sending shock-waves of despair through the entire community and creating a faultline in Fraser and Caitlin's friendship

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, Caitlin prepares to leave the base and move back to Chicago with her family. But she and Fraser had made plans to sneak off to Blood Orange concert in Bologna together, and Fraser insists they should go ahead with the trip

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore three properties, including a run-down bungalow, an end terrace with no upstairs bathroom, and a centuries-old cottage

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The eland herd is growing fast after the introduction of a young male, while Jean Johansson discovers she helped kick-start the biggest flock of newborn flamingos in years

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A retired serviceman who drove a burning boat out of a Washington marina to prevent the flames spreading, and the rescue of a mountain climber who fell 20ft down a rock face

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Brighton Racecourse, with experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey helping the teams. Charlie Ross also visits the National Horseracing Museum

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi questions what really happened to Tom and Ella's mother. Daniel is shocked by the identity of a violent prisoner, and Ruhma gets the chance to make peace with the past

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps buyers who want to relocate from Surrey to north Devon on a £650,000 budget, and visits the annual regatta in the fishing village of Appledore

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon search for antiques on the Cornish coast, where Izzie picks up some Scandinavian jewellery, and Catherine finds a Bristol Blue glass lamp stand

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Dementia Choir with Vicky McClure
programme.desc: Just over two years ago, Vicky McClure took to the stage at the Royal Concert Hall in Nottingham with her choir of people all living with dementia to perform in front of 2000 people. Now, this heart-warming programme tells the story of Vicky dementia choir through the Covid-19 lockdown, leading up to a very special Christmas performance for one choir member

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore four more treasured family heirlooms, tackling a musical wooden bench, a Victorian kaleidoscope, military items owned by a soldier who served in both world wars and a pair of Royal Crown Derby peacocks

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The remaining four chefs embark on the penultimate round of the competition, cooking for some of the most respected chefs in the UK at the Chefs Table. The chefs have three hours and 15 minutes to create a spectacular dish and get it to the pass ready to serve, hoping to wow the judges and earn praise from the best in the business. In the second round, the competitors are challenged to create a dish of their own design dedicated to someone special in their life, before one of them is sent home

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield and Leicester City v Everton at Goodison Park. The Reds and Spurs were among the early-season pacesetters, and will both have been looking to put a marker down, while Leicester and Everton both have aspirations of qualifying for Europe next term. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and Tuesday's matches

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time investor takes on a garden level flat in London's Crystal Palace, Dion Dublin meets a lorry driver who's renovating a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on Trent, and in Strood, Kent, a Navy reservist transforms a three-storey townhouse into a stylish holiday let

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The lions need a new scratching post and Jean Johansson finds out exactly what happens at a meerkat Christmas party

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition for BBC Music Day, which sees the Darkness frontman Justin Hawkins and bass guitarist Frankie Poullain competing against Feeder singer Grant Nicholas and bassist Taka Hirose. They'll be scouring an antiques fair in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, with the help of Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell, in a bid to make the most cash - and while they're busy, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the BBC's famous Maida Vale studios

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al takes Jimmi, Emma and Valerie on a spooky Christmas outing where they end up confronting some of their own personal demons

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple hunting for a home in Derbyshire on a budget of £380,000, and meets traditional dairy producers reviving a village's cheese-making heritage

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon's search takes them to Devon, where items include a 1920s flapper dress, violins, trinket boxes and a walking stick with a maritime connection

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki ignites a war and Jack delivers some unwanted news. As Tina's trust in Gray wavers, she seeks out answers

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain continues her culinary journey with a visit to California, home to one of the most diverse populations in America. She is invited to a Mexican quinceanera - an extravagant fiesta celebrating a girl's 15th birthday - cooks for an all-woman daredevil rodeo riding team and makes handmade fortune cookies with a grandma in one of the last remaining workshops in San Francisco's Chinatown

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2020 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. They are given three hours and 15 minutes to cook a starter, a main course and a dessert, each of which takes their culinary abilities to a whole new level, with flawless presentation and exciting flavours. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Gregg Wallace assess their efforts, before deciding the winner

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund and Lord Melchett are embarrassingly kidnapped by a maniacal German master of disguise with designs on the English throne. Unfortunately for them, the Queen decides to pay only one of the ransom demands. Comedy, guest starring Hugh Laurie, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conor's mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble go behind the scenes at Longleat House and Safari Park once more. The keepers work hard to get the animals ready for winter, and also introduce new residents, including binturongs, dik-diks and ostriches

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer takes on Catherine Southon as they search for items in the antiques shops along the south coast of England on their way to an auction in Leicestershire

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a physics student, a facilities manager and a customer advisor must choose between working together to pool their knowledge or going it alone

programme.title: King of Kings (1961)
programme.desc: Biblical epic chronicling the life of Christ, from the rumours of a coming Messiah before his birth, through his rise as a spiritual leader to his eventual betrayal, crucifixion and resurrection. Narrated by Orson Welles and starring Jeffrey Hunter, Siobhan McKenna, Robert Ryan, Rip Torn, Hurd Hatfield and Harry Guardino

programme.title: The Snow Wolf Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to Canada in the autumn to reunite with the Arctic wolves he has been following as they try to raise their pups and prepare for winter. The cameraman also walks with members of the pack as they hunt lemmings, musk oxen and hares and encounter a rival pack

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Memorable moments and previously unseen finds, including a handkerchief commemorating cricket legend Dr WG Grace and three postcard albums found on a council tip

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball talks to Jamie Laing and Karen Hauer, who managed to avoid elimination on Sunday and took the last spot in the final. Plus, a closer look at the weekend's performances in Choreography Corner, and previous winners Stacey Dooley, Kelvin Fletcher and Harry Judd join Zoe to review the series

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman visit places where traditional Christmas items roll off the production line. Gregg goes to Britain's biggest cake factory, where 180 million mince pies are produced each year, and pitches in on the yule log conveyor belt. Cherry finds out where tinsel and wrapping paper are made and Ruth learns the history of Christmas crackers

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Simon Rogan and Tommy Banks join the desserts and petit fours round, in which the eight contestants prepare dishes including edible snow globes, mince pies, baked Alaska, and a new taker on the classic Christmas selection box. They must also judge each other's efforts in a blind taste test. Former judge Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: Highlights from past Christmas episodes of the panel show, with host Sandi Toksvig and regular panellist Alan Davies

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Harper reveals details of Eric's indiscretions to Daria, setting in motion a chain of events with serious ramifications. Yasmin tries to get Maxim to come to the Christmas party, but he struggles to see past what Kenny did at the strip club. Greg is left in an unstable state after being publicly humiliated, and his state of mind soon escalates when Usman shows up with some grade A narcotics

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A selection of hit performances from the 1960s to the present day

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: During late summer and autumn, the Royal Free London has so far managed to remain open to all its patients and continue with planned operations, treatment and care. However, the second wave of the pandemic has now reached London, and confirmed cases of the virus are prompting a fresh influx of patients with Covid-19 into the hospitals. As a second England-wide lockdown is in force, the Royal Free Hospital must continue to try to get through the biggest operating waiting list in its history. As a specialist centre, it sees some of the UK's most complicated cancer patients. But with many patients in need of intensive care beds, the pressure placed on the Trust's intensive care staff is immense. Meanwhile, in Barnet, the trust's second largest hospital, the number of Covid-19 patients has more than doubled in the last week. As the hospital deals with the new influx of patients, Barnet's senior team have a major staffing problem. Because of an outbreak of Covid-19 on three of the wards, st

programme.title: Their Finest (2016)
programme.desc: Former secretary Catrin Cole, newly appointed by the British Ministry of Information as a scriptwriter for propaganda films, joins the cast and crew of a major production while the Blitz rages around them. Comedy drama, starring Gemma Arterton, Sam Claflin, Richard E Grant and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In October 1917, the Bolsheviks rose up, swept the Russian tsar from power and communism was born. However, the exact facts of the Russian Revolution are disputed. Here, the historian finds it was really a group of women workers who kick-started the event nine months earlier. At the time, the Bolsheviks tried to stop it, and Lenin - radical leader of the Bolsheviks - wasn't even in the country. Lucy also discovers that the tsar was forced to abdicate long before the Bolsheviks took control and that King George V betrayed his cousin by opposing the British government's offer of asylum to the tsar

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. First, Monica asks two chefs to fillet and cook john dory and serve with a vibrant green parsley sauce and a garlic cream, before the second pair of competitors make poached figs with honeycomb and a flavoured cream for Marcus. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: New binturong Baloo is settling into its enclosure and awaiting a first meeting with a new mate, but the Christmas tryst does not go to plan as the boisterous animal escapes

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer finds some 18th-century porcelain, and Catherine Southon acquires some Scandinavian silver and samples an ancient pasty recipe before heading to an auction in Devon

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the silk and sequins at a major bhangra dance contest in Birmingham. It follows two rival teams as they shed blood, sweat and tears in their quest to win the coveted trophy

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Nurse Leanne responds to a medical emergency when a pupil has a seizure and is having difficulty breathing, while hairdresser Claire volunteers her time in the school's salon to work with pupils who find it extremely difficult and sometimes painful to have their hair cut

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which two students and a retired GP must choose between working together to pool their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Nigel Slater's 12 Tastes of Christmas
programme.desc: The food writer presents the second and final programme of tips for a special foodie yuletide. He demonstrates how people can spoil themselves on Christmas Day and beyond with recipes for centrepieces and sweet treats, and makes clever use of leftovers. He also continues his journey in snowy Scandinavia to find out how Norwegians take full advantage of festive flavours

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the TV appearances made over the years by Hollywood star Robert Mitchum, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career

programme.title: Holiday Affair (1949)
programme.desc: A department store clerk discovers a war widow is doing market research for a rival shop, but when he does not report her to the management, it costs him his job just before Christmas. They meet again when he carries out a further act of kindness toward her son, and romance develops, despite her impending marriage. Comedy, starring Janet Leigh and Robert Mitchum

programme.title: The Polar Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels to the Norwegian archipelago of Svalbard to document the lives of a polar bear and her two cubs over the course of three seasons. In spring, he and a team of arctic survival experts manage to find a birth den and meet mother Lyra and offspring Miki and Luca as they emerge from it. Gordon then helps scientists fit the adult with a tracking collar and tests a bear-proof filming hide as he takes on the mission of living with the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, a place bursting with remnants from the town's industrial past. Elizabeth Talbot and James Lewis find the best antiques and collectibles to take to auction from the assembled crowd, while Paul visits the home of the man now considered to be the father of ecology, 18th-century naturalist Gilbert White

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long go test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball shares the news and gossip from Strictly as one of the final couples joins her on the sofa. Ian Waite casts his expert eye over all the couples's training footage

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman explore the fascinating factory processes and surprising history behind the nation's favourite festive treats. Gregg follows 24 hours of production at a factory in Oldham, Greater Manchester, which will be making two million Christmas cakes for Marks and Spencer, while Cherry visits Britain's largest marzipan manufacturer as well as a sprout farm. Ruth investigates how the UK's early industrial heritage inspired Charles Dickens to write A Christmas Carol

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the dessert and petit fours heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. This week, actor and comedian Kerry Godliman and regular judges Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton are joined by special guest judge, Beverly Knight MBE. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: The King's Speech (2010)
programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. The support and friendship of the doctor prove invaluable when a crisis places the repressed prince on the throne, just as the outbreak of the Second World War leaves the country in need of a strong king. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Geoffrey Rush, Helena Bonham Carter and Guy Pearce

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: George VI: The Reluctant King
programme.desc: A profile of the monarch, who reluctantly acceded to the throne after his brother Edward's abdication. The new king overcame shyness, reticence and a debilitating stammer to lead the nation through the Second World War, before his death at the age of 56. Narrated by Andrew Sachs

programme.title: The Duchess (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based period drama, chronicling the life of Georgiana Spencer who became Duchess of Devonshire in 1774 at the age of 17. Adored by the public for her extravagant lifestyle, Spencer endured a troubled marriage and was eventually driven into the arms of Charles Grey, a senior Whig politician. Starring Keira Knightley, Ralph Fiennes, Dominic Cooper and Charlotte Rampling

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom visits the Black Bull in the village of Gartmore in Stirling. Struggling to survive, it was put on the market and 250 locals clubbed together to buy it. Now the pub is staffed entirely by volunteers and the management are planning to renovate the bedrooms to attract tourists. But Tom has his concerns. He also catches up with the staff of the pubs he is helping in Cornwall, south London and Stroud - but just when all the hard work seems to be paying off, the pandemic strikes. Now every pub in Britain faces a fight for survival

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: Waterholes are vital to the African ecosystem, but little is known about how they support so much life. Chris Packham and Ella Al-Shamahi work with Mwiba Wildlife Reserve in Tanzania to build the world's first waterhole with a built-in specialist camera rig

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, where they are challenged to create a dish that captures the essence of the forest, making the most of ingredients from meat to foraging produce such as mushrooms and berries. They then have to cook two courses for restaurant critics Grace Dent and Jay Rayner, who scrutinise the dishes in each and every detail. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The eland herd is growing fast after the introduction of a young male, while Jean Johansson discovers she helped kick-start the biggest flock of newborn flamingos in years

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon search for antiques on the Cornish coast, where Izzie picks up some Scandinavian jewellery, and Catherine finds a Bristol Blue glass lamp stand

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of first-time puppy owners. Rescue dog Elina moves in with the Orchard family in Buckinghamshire after a 2,000-mile trip from Cyprus, where she was found abandoned on the side of the road. Meanwhile, Sage the Staffordshire bull terrier cross is a massive hit with the Fianko family

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Victoria, Yasmin and Jordon must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of TV interviews with actor David Niven, examining the appearances on chat shows he made over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Bishop's Wife (1947)
programme.desc: Distraught bishop Henry is struggling to cope with building a new cathedral, but receives help from on high in the form of a suave guardian angel. The heavenly visitor proves influential in fundraising, but when he turns his attention to Henry's family life, the cleric suspects his new friend has designs on his wife. Fantasy, starring Cary Grant, David Niven and Loretta Young

programme.title: The Polar Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan and the team return to Svalbard in mid-June on the trail of Lyra and her two cubs to find out how they are coping with the unusual Arctic weather. Gordon locates the family and renews his bond with the adult bear, but discovers that cub Luca is missing and documents the struggle to survive as the mother bear tries to keep her remaining offspring safe

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Caroline Hawley head to the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall, where they unearth items including an unusual tortoise and an electric guitar

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Steve Cram, Lloyd Griffith, Jeanette Kwakye and Josie Long test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the finalists' friends and family, who share their thoughts on how their loved ones will do, and designer Vicky Gill appears one last time to reveal some of Saturday night's costumes

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a factory that produces two million tins of festive chocolate assortments a year, with 5,500 individual sweets packaged and dropped into containers every minute. Cherry Healey learns how the Royal Mail produces Christmas stamps, and also travels to Germany to join a crew of 35 ornament decorators applying glitter and paint to glass Santas. Historian Ruth Goodman goes on the trail of the festive turkey, a tradition that began in the 16th century when these birds were first introduced from Mexico

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Linus the Bedlington terrier joins the Seymour family, but mum Rebecca is not sure she has made the right decision. Cookie the French bulldog moves in with hairdresser Louise, personal trainer Phil and their two young boys. The family have high hopes she will fit in with their active lifestyle, but are thrown when she refuses to go out on walks. In n West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo has now been with retirees Linda and Andrew for a month and shows no signs of slowing down

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The final documentary looks at one of the greatest challenges faced by modern Elizabethans - the loss of Britain's manufacturing heartlands and the surprising impact it has had on the state of the nation. Andrew Marr selects a rich cast of characters, from Christopher Cockerell to Clive Sinclair, who in his opinion have responded to the challenges and found ingenious ways of adapting to the changing industrial landscape

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig has a Christmas knees-up in the pub with Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Josh Widdicombe and Noel Fielding. Hostelry-themed questions are the order of the day, with a sprinkling of festive cheer along the way

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Everyone Romesh has ever met has told him how amazing the Scottish islands are - but nobody he knows has actually ever been there. Now, in a year when travel abroad has been all but forbidden, he is heading to the Hebrides to find out if you can really have a proper adventure so close to home, spending a week visiting the isles of Skye, Harris and Lewis. Guiding him through the journey will be Donald Macsween, or Sweeney to his friends, a local crofter from the far north of Lewis - a place that lies as far north as Greenland. During their trip, Sweeney will show Romesh the spectacular rugged beauty of the islands and what at first glance look like Caribbean sandy beaches (but turn out to be utterly freezing). Rom will try his hand at the Highlands Games and try to avoid feeling up a sheep's testicles during a day spent on Sweeney's farm. Along the way, there will be a trip to the remote island of St Kilda - four hours out across the open Atlantic - and a chaotic afternoon spent with lo

programme.title: An Education (2009)
programme.desc: A hard-working, intelligent 16-year-old is on her way to gaining a place at Oxford University. However, her conventional education is called into question when an older, worldly suitor takes her under his wing and exposes her to some of the finer things in life. Coming-of-age drama set in the 1960s and based on Lynn Barber's memoir, with Carey Mulligan, Peter Sarsgaard, Alfred Molina, Cara Seymour and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Russell Kane and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the revival of Doctor Who, and Paula Radcliffe winning the London Marathon.

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver oversees proceedings as previous winners of the culinary contest prepare a feast for the heroes of the pandemic - beginning with a course of canapes. In a twist on previous series, the eight cooks will be split into two teams of four and will judge the other group's efforts in a blind taste test. Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen will be joining the teams in the kitchen

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the canape and starter heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. With Andi Oliver hosting proceedings, there is a vacancy in the judging chamber, so joining Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton is actor and comedian Kerry Godliman

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman chairs the latest round of the celebrity quiz, which pits famous alumni of Warwick University and Imperial College London against each other. Representing Warwick are actor Tom Goodman-Hill and The World Tonight presenter Ritula Shah, while the Imperial team is engineer Brendan Walker and writer and broadcaster Kenan Malik

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a winter scene, using shades of indigo create the stark reality of the season

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Hacker attempts to gain support for his tax cuts - by threatening to raise the duty on tobacco. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: A territorial battle develops in the corridors of power. Sir Humphrey has skilfully moved the PM's political adviser Dorothy Wainwright out of her office - but she insists on moving back. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary looks at how Margaret Thatcher's leadership style and the issues of Europe and the poll tax split her cabinet and created public anger, leading to her downfall. Protests against the poll tax resulted in violence in central London, but more damagingly there was widespread disenchantment in Tory strongholds across the country. Fractures emerge with senior cabinet colleagues, worsened by her European policy and Geoffrey Howe's resignation leads to a leadership challenge

programme.title: Great Speeches
programme.desc: Conservative MP Geoffrey Howe's influential addressing of the House of Commons in 1990, 12 days after his resignation from the government, a speech many believe shook the nation and precipitated the downfall of Margaret Thatcher

programme.title: Diary Days
programme.desc: An insight into life on November 22, 1990, when Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher resigned

programme.title: Favourite Things: Margaret Thatcher
programme.desc: Russell Harty meets the former prime minister to discuss her life, interests and personal possessions

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark Kermode celebrates one of the most perennial of all movie genres - the Christmas film. He unwraps a glittering selection of festive cinematic treats, from Hollywood blockbusters to family favourites, revealing the techniques and storytelling secrets that make them so successful. Touching on styles ranging from fairy-tale fantasy to high-octane action, family drama to horror, Mark considers how the best of these films are able to capture something magical - the Christmas spirit. But how do they do it?

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary asking why the festive meal remains the nation's last great traditional feast, and if it can continue to stand the test of time in an increasingly diverse society. With contributions by actor Simon Callow, food writer Prue Leith, cultural critic Jonathan Meades and historians Kate Colquhoun and Kathryn Hughes

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Adaptation of John Le Carre's novel, starring Tom Hiddleston, Olivia Colman and Hugh Laurie. A former soldier working in a Cairo hotel discovers evidence against international arms dealer Richard Roper, but his attempt to help British intelligence to capture the criminal has disastrous consequences. Five years later, he is based in Switzerland, working at a hotel that he discovers is regularly frequented by Roper. Determined to make amends for his previous failure, he reunites with his contact at British intelligence and they form a plan to infiltrate the arms dealer's inner circle

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Pine and Burr hatch a plan to get Roper's attention by setting up a series of faked criminal activities, including stealing from a hotel and conducting drug deals in Devon. Their intention is for Pine to develop a reputation in the underworld, which could give him the chance to infiltrate the arms dealer's inner circle. Six months later, Roper's son is kidnapped by two thieves from his villa in Mallorca - but the abduction is not what it seems, nor is the mysterious figure lurking in the shadows

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a winter scene, using shades of indigo create the stark reality of the season

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary looks at how Margaret Thatcher's leadership style and the issues of Europe and the poll tax split her cabinet and created public anger, leading to her downfall. Protests against the poll tax resulted in violence in central London, but more damagingly there was widespread disenchantment in Tory strongholds across the country. Fractures emerge with senior cabinet colleagues, worsened by her European policy and Geoffrey Howe's resignation leads to a leadership challenge

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the first semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series. Hosted by Jeremy Paxman

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint the first snowfall after autumn, which signals the true beginning of winter

programme.title: Wartime Farm
programme.desc: Historian Ruth Goodman and archaeologist Peter Ginn return to Manor Farm in Hampshire to recreate the conditions that prevailed in rural Britain during the Christmas of 1944. Peter calls upon crafts expert Colin Richards to help brew some improvised potato beer, while Ruth comes up with innovative presents for children and ingenious festive decorations made from scraps. Following in the footsteps of many wartime farmers, they transport their gifts, food and beer on a vintage steam train to Chislehurst Caves, where they discover what Christmas was like for some of the 15,000 people who sheltered there. Plus, Ruth cooks a yuletide meal from recipes and guidelines issued by the government and the Women's Voluntary Service

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Christmas Carol Odyssey
programme.desc: Lucy explores the tradition of festive hymns, revealing that there is much more to the best-loved carols than meets the eye, and how they reveal the history of Christmas itself. In the ancient past, pagan fertility ritual the wassail gave rise to door-to-door singing, and was also an integral part of an older midwinter festival adopted by Christianity when it came to Britain, and was rebranded as Christmas

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A collection of chart-toppers from festive episodes of the music show, including the first-ever repeat showing of a recently rediscovered 1967 performance by the Rolling Stones. The programme also features perennial Christmas classics from the likes of Slade, Mud and Frankie Goes to Hollywood, as well as Ian Dury & the Blockheads, Madness, East 17, Coldplay, the Human League and the Pet Shop Boys

programme.title: The Sound of TV with Neil Brand
programme.desc: With the advent of commercial television in the UK, ITV rivalled the BBC for airtime and lured viewers in with the new language of advertisements. However, these had not yet reached the impact they had seen in the USA, the home of the TV jingle, where a 30-second tune could make or break a brand, as seen in the competing fortunes of Coca-Cola and Pepsi. Neil reveals the lasting musical power of jingles, idents and advertisements, including the long-forgotten test card tunes sampled in American hip-hop

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Apostol throws a lavish party to celebrate his daughter's birthday - but when the guest of honour is found dead, he becomes vulnerable and Burr is able to gain leverage over him. Roper digs deeper into Pine's fictitious criminal past and offers him a job in his organisation - but Corky remains suspicious of the newcomer. When Pine is given permission to take Daniel into town, he and Burr have a chance to share intelligence and deal with a potential threat to their plans by persuading the troubled Apostol to turn on Corky

programme.title: The Night Manager
programme.desc: Pine is now firmly embedded in Roper's organisation and gets involved in an arms deal, but his relationship with Jed, and Corky's growing jealousy, jeopardise his cover. Burr puts pressure on Apostol to help her translate the information she has received on Roper's offshore operation, but begins to suspect the arms dealer has been buying weapons from the British government - a fear that Palfrey confirms. A tip-off leads her to Istanbul, but her attempt to catch Roper in the act puts Pine in further danger

programme.title: Wartime Farm
programme.desc: Historian Ruth Goodman and archaeologist Peter Ginn return to Manor Farm in Hampshire to recreate the conditions that prevailed in rural Britain during the Christmas of 1944. Peter calls upon crafts expert Colin Richards to help brew some improvised potato beer, while Ruth comes up with innovative presents for children and ingenious festive decorations made from scraps. Following in the footsteps of many wartime farmers, they transport their gifts, food and beer on a vintage steam train to Chislehurst Caves, where they discover what Christmas was like for some of the 15,000 people who sheltered there. Plus, Ruth cooks a yuletide meal from recipes and guidelines issued by the government and the Women's Voluntary Service

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross uses striking blue tones to produce a night-time winter scene, complete with his trademark cabin, inside an oval surround

programme.title: Far from the Madding Crowd (2015)
programme.desc: A headstrong Victorian woman inherits a sheep farm in a remote area of Dorset. She struggles to maintain her independence while being courted by three very different men - a stoic farmer, a dashing, reckless soldier and a lonely older man. Period drama based on Thomas Hardy's novel, starring Carey Mulligan, Matthias Schoenaerts, Tom Sturridge and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A tribute to the renowned London-born pop diva, featuring BBC archive footage of some of Dusty's most famous performances from 1961 to 1995. The programme also offers a chance to relive the singer's story, from her folk beginnings with the Springfields, through to her Motown homage years and her collaboration with Pet Shop Boys in the late 1980s. Also includes the star's duets with Tom Jones and Mel Torme

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Pop diva Dusty Springfield hosts the final edition of her 1960s entertainment showcase, chatting to Scott Walker of the Walker Brothers, and performing her chart-topping 1966 single You Don't Have to Say You Love Me. Originally broadcast in 1967

programme.title: Tamara Drewe (2010)
programme.desc: A woman returns to the sleepy village where she grew up, having transformed from an awkward teenager to a successful and confident journalist. She instantly becomes the object of obsession for every man in the neighbourhood, while her sex life becomes a source of fascination for two local girls. Comedy based on Posy Simmonds' comic strip, with Gemma Arterton, Dominic Cooper and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: The Night Manager

programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep hosts a Christmas quiz featuring funny festive clips and a seasonal singalong. Professor Muddles and Ann Teak join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt as they attempt to win not just Gigglepoints but some special Christmas Gigglepresents too. The first round is a simple clip round with a question for each team. The second round kicks off with Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, featuring Nana’s cows in a Christmassy Kitchen attempting to make messy Christmas puddings. Then it's on to the third and final buzzer round, and this is Gail and Keith’s last chance to win their Gigglepresents. They both get ready on their buzzers as Arthur reveals a special previously unseen festive clip, featuring DIY Dan clumsily trying to decorate a Christmas tree. Professor Muddles has a bright idea to end the show on a singalong. And so, the teams gather round to sing us out with a lovely rendition of We Wish You a Giggle Quizmas.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: It’s our big Christmas special! programme.title: Land of the Wild: After Dark
programme.desc: Cutting-edge camera technology reveals the secrets of Wales's nocturnal wildlife. Filmed across the seasons, from the biting cold of winter to sweltering summer nights, a variety of innovative cameras capture wild action between sunrise and sunset, from swimming hedgehogs and fishing bats, to super moons and comets. Military-grade long lens thermal cameras, which record heat rather than light, film 20,000 starlings racing to roost under Aberystwyth's pier and bats hunting insects over Margam Lake. Super-sensitive cameras which film at night in colour reveal the nocturnal secrets of badgers, moths, hedgehogs, toads and owls, and infrared light illuminates 300 lesser horseshoe bats leaving their castle roost

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The remaining four chefs embark on the penultimate round of the competition, cooking for some of the most respected chefs in the UK at the Chefs Table. The chefs have three hours and 15 minutes to create a spectacular dish and get it to the pass ready to serve, hoping to wow the judges and earn praise from the best in the business. In the second round, the competitors are challenged to create a dish of their own design dedicated to someone special in their life, before one of them is sent home

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conor's mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.desc: Edmund and Lord Melchett are embarrassingly kidnapped by a maniacal German master of disguise with designs on the English throne. Unfortunately for them, the Queen decides to pay only one of the ransom demands. Comedy, guest starring Hugh Laurie, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Fortitude star Robert Sheehan appears as himself in this festive episode of the sitcom. The residents of the council estate where Conor and Jock live are threatened with eviction, but are not about to go down without a fight - and the resulting media circus puts everyone on edge. In the midst of all this mayhem, Conor's mother Mairead is rushed to hospital, and may be more seriously ill than previously thought. When she says she wants to see a white Christmas, the lads vow to make her wish come true - with a little help from Sheehan, who is shooting a film nearby

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit properties in Kent, Derby and Stoke-on-Trent, all of which have different renovation issues to resolve before they can be sold

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: New binturong Baloo is settling into its enclosure and awaiting a first meeting with a new mate, but the Christmas tryst does not go to plan as the boisterous animal escapes

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A man fights off masked house raiders to save his wife, a manta ray faces certain death if a fish hook is not removed, and in America, drivers stop to save a woman in a flash flood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presides at two antiques centres in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, as two teams of married couples go head to head. John Cameron leads the reds, while Caroline Hawley takes charge of the blues. The bargain hunters have to spend £300 on three items within an hour in the hope they'll make a profit at the auction in Suffolk. Meanwhile, Anita investigates the story behind an unusual toy

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie throws Lily a Bollywood-themed farewell party as Emma's plans for a romantic evening unravel and Rob and Karen try to convince Tom and Ella to accept help

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a Kent family find a new home in the East Riding of Yorkshire with room for a catering business, and takes a trip on a traditional fishing boat in Bridlington

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne use his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre in Monmouthshire, searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A collection of trampoline poles are worked on by carpenter Ollie Allen, while lighting specialist Guy Trench has to come up with a bright idea for a paraffin can and a pair of blow torches. For his own project, EJ has a large desk to keep him busy

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer finds some 18th-century porcelain, and Catherine Southon acquires some Scandinavian silver and samples an ancient pasty recipe before heading to an auction in Devon

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Revelations threaten to rock Stuart and Rainie. Tina unravels under the mounting pressure. Kim falls at the final hurdle while Denise insists on a traditional Christmas for Raymond

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian's commitment to going cold turkey is tested when Sarah Jane calls in a favour, and he finds himself tempted to crack under the pressure. Jodie feels pushed aside when Sacha's daughter starts asking questions about his surprise engagement, but refuses to give up without a fight. Nicky does not know who to believe when Chloe accuses Cameron of abusing his position

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: The level of competitiveness and camaraderie grows as comedian Roisin Conaty, ex-footballer Jermaine Jenas, comedian Jason Manford and comedy actress Jennifer Saunders return to test their general knowledge skills in more fun trivia-based games

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Finals week gets underway with the last Invention Test of the year. The six remaining contestants are divided into two teams, one of which prepares rabbit in mustard sauce, while the other makes bouillabaisse with a rouille and a crouton. The chef who presents the finest dish in each of the groups will be rewarded with a pass straight through to the final four, while the others must make a classic dessert to avoid elimination

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary looks back on a dramatic 12 months. As the pandemic remains a challenge to governments across the world, she hears from the Taoiseach Micheál Martin and, with a panel of commentators, asks if the virus has pulled relationships at the Stormont Executive together or further apart

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: News of a terrible event reaches the US base in Chioggia, sending shock-waves of despair through the entire community and creating a faultline in Fraser and Caitlin's friendship

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, Caitlin prepares to leave the base and move back to Chicago with her family. But she and Fraser had made plans to sneak off to Blood Orange concert in Bologna together, and Fraser insists they should go ahead with the trip

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore three properties, including a run-down bungalow, an end terrace with no upstairs bathroom, and a centuries-old cottage

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The eland herd is growing fast after the introduction of a young male, while Jean Johansson discovers she helped kick-start the biggest flock of newborn flamingos in years

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A retired serviceman who drove a burning boat out of a Washington marina to prevent the flames spreading, and the rescue of a mountain climber who fell 20ft down a rock face

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Brighton Racecourse, with experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey helping the teams. Charlie Ross also visits the National Horseracing Museum

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi questions what really happened to Tom and Ella's mother. Daniel is shocked by the identity of a violent prisoner, and Ruhma gets the chance to make peace with the past

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps buyers who want to relocate from Surrey to north Devon on a £650,000 budget, and visits the annual regatta in the fishing village of Appledore

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Catherine Southon search for antiques on the Cornish coast, where Izzie picks up some Scandinavian jewellery, and Catherine finds a Bristol Blue glass lamp stand

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present

programme.title: Our Dementia Choir with Vicky McClure
programme.desc: Just over two years ago, Vicky McClure took to the stage at the Royal Concert Hall in Nottingham with her choir of people all living with dementia to perform in front of 2000 people. Now, this heart-warming programme tells the story of Vicky dementia choir through the Covid-19 lockdown, leading up to a very special Christmas performance for one choir member

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore four more treasured family heirlooms, tackling a musical wooden bench, a Victorian kaleidoscope, military items owned by a soldier who served in both world wars and a pair of Royal Crown Derby peacocks

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The remaining four chefs embark on the penultimate round of the competition, cooking for some of the most respected chefs in the UK at the Chefs Table. The chefs have three hours and 15 minutes to create a spectacular dish and get it to the pass ready to serve, hoping to wow the judges and earn praise from the best in the business. In the second round, the competitors are challenged to create a dish of their own design dedicated to someone special in their life, before one of them is sent home

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield and Leicester City v Everton at Goodison Park. The Reds and Spurs were among the early-season pacesetters, and will both have been looking to put a marker down, while Leicester and Everton both have aspirations of qualifying for Europe next term. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and Tuesday's matches

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an extended edition of the quiz, with two guests joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-time investor takes on a garden level flat in London's Crystal Palace, Dion Dublin meets a lorry driver who's renovating a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on Trent, and in Strood, Kent, a Navy reservist transforms a three-storey townhouse into a stylish holiday let

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: The lions need a new scratching post and Jean Johansson finds out exactly what happens at a meerkat Christmas party

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition for BBC Music Day, which sees the Darkness frontman Justin Hawkins and bass guitarist Frankie Poullain competing against Feeder singer Grant Nicholas and bassist Taka Hirose. They'll be scouring an antiques fair in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire, with the help of Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell, in a bid to make the most cash - and while they're busy, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the BBC's famous Maida Vale studios

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Witley Recycling Centre, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Ollie Allen helping to transform a chair, a wooden chest and a medieval-style tent

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki ignites a war and Jack delivers some unwanted news. As Tina's trust in Gray wavers, she seeks out answers

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain continues her culinary journey with a visit to California, home to one of the most diverse populations in America. She is invited to a Mexican quinceanera - an extravagant fiesta celebrating a girl's 15th birthday - cooks for an all-woman daredevil rodeo riding team and makes handmade fortune cookies with a grandma in one of the last remaining workshops in San Francisco's Chinatown

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2020 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: On the 70th anniversary of one of her career-defining films All about Eve, Talking Movies looks back at the life and times of Bette Davis, regarded as one of the greatest actresses in Hollywood history. Davis worked for more than 60 years and made over 100 films, including classics such as Whatever Happened to Baby Jane? and Dark Victory. She was a trailblazer for the independent actress who feuded with studio bosses at a time when women in Hollywood were not expected to be assertive. The programme includes of one of the last major interviews Davis gave on a film set to Talking Movies host Tom Brook, filmed on location in Maine where she was shooting her penultimate film, The Whales of August, which co-starred silent screen icon Lillian Gish. This tribute to Davis also includes interviews with younger actresses who still regard Davis as an inspirational force and an assessment of her body of work from a range of critics, film-makers, producers and her many fans. programme.title: Beethoven: A’ Chuirm Ghàidhlig
programme.desc: When piper Allan MacDonald revealed Beethoven's song Enchantress, Farewell was a reworking of Gaelic song Mnathan á Ghlinne Seo, German Gaelic-learner Michael Klevenhaus turned detective. We follow his quest to discover more works by the maestro based on traditional music of Gaeldom. Michael reveals that the melodies' origins were deliberately hidden from Beethoven and the public by Edinburgh publisher George Thomson, who commissioned the arrangements from the composer. With the 1745 Jacobite Rebellion still within living memory, Thomson wanted to strip the Gaelic songs of their titles, lyrics and the ‘taint' of Jacobitism. Discovering a wealth of Beethoven's forgotten Gaelic songs, Michael and Allan MacDonald stage a concert featuring both Beethoven's and the ancient Gaelic versions

programme.title: Beethoven: A’ Chuirm Ghàidhlig
programme.desc: The discovery that Beethoven's arrangement of the song Enchantress Farewell was a reworking of a Gaelic song, Mhnathan a' Ghlinne Seo, led German scholar Michael Klevenhaus to search for more connections between old Gaelic melodies and the great German composer. That search featured in the recent documentary Òrain Ghàidhlig Beethoven (Beethoven's Gaelic Songs). This concert, featuring both traditional and classical musicians, highlights more of the Gaelic songs that Michael discovered had found their way to Beethoven and the classical repertoire. The featured musicians are: Traditional singers Mairi MacInnes and Michael Klevenhaus. Scots piper Allan MacDonald and German piper Thomas Zöllner. Scottish classical singers Lorna Anderson (soprano) and Jamie MacDougall (tenor). And the classical instrumentalists Verena Stourzh, Franz Ortner, Clemens Zeilinger of the Trio Van Beethoven

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Steve, Jennifer, Amy and Paul pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: An apprehensive Jai and Laurel arrive at the hospital, Gabby talks to Arthur, and Will is concerned about Harriet in the wake of recent news

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters and other presenters explore the islands and highlands of the Scottish countryside. Julia Bradbury hikes the West Highland Way, and Robson Green dives for scallops off the Isle of Skye. At the historic Edinburgh castle, Rory Bremner finds an unusual way to set his watch, and in the waters around the Isle of Mull, Liz Bonnin gets up close with some rare sea creatures

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: The series concludes with tips on buying food and drink, starting with alternatives to turkey for Christmas dinner - including meat, fish and vegetarian options. Matt Tebbutt offers advice on the best value for money when giving gift hampers, and The Great British Bake Off's Bryony May Williams judges a gingerbread baking contest. Grace Dent challenges members of the public to tell the difference between luxury and supermarket food, and the stars of The Only Way Is Essex offer tips on making cocktails

programme.title: The Real Full Monty: On Ice
programme.desc: Part two of two. The two teams of celebrities trained by Ashley Banjo and Coleen Nolan are finally ready to take to the ice and perform a naked dancing extravaganza in the fourth annual charity event promoting the importance of cancer checks. There is no room for error for the dancers, as the tiniest mistake could end in catastrophe

programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. This time, Brenda Blethyn has a run-in with an amorous puppy, a Downing Street reporter swallows a fly and Stephen Mulhern faces a potty-mouthed member of the public. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Great British Escape
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul heads down Kent's coastline to Botany Bay to discover more about tales of smuggling. Here, he meets locals Pete and George, who tell him how the soft, chalky white cliffs around Dover were perfect for smugglers to drill tunnels where they could hide their treasures. Later, Paul scales the great tower at the iconic Dover Castle, before making his way to Port Lympne Reserve, one of the UK's leading wildlife conservation centres

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: After last year's trip to the unlikely festive destination of Morocco, Gordon Ramsay is determined to celebrate Christmas properly this year, arranging a trip to the ultimate winter wonderland of Lapland. Gino D'Acampo has a letter he wants to hand deliver to Santa from his daughter Mia, Fred is keen to witness the once-in-a-lifetime wonder of the Northern Lights and Gordon is eager that they all encounter the culinary delights of the country. But along the way, they face plenty of distractions, including ice swimming, saunas, snowmobiles and snowballing

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: With just over a week to go until Christmas Day, Martin Lewis has tips on how to make money go further, with advice on consumer rights and family budgets. He and Anjellica Bell also have the latest financial news and Martin's Big Briefing

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary following life in the glamorous London hotel over the festive season, as staff prepare to welcome guests during the hotel's most profitable time of the year. Head butler Sean braces new recruit Michael for how busy he is going to be, while restaurant director Anne prepares the four restaurants and her 160 staff. Cameras also follow regular guest Howell Harris as he spends his first Christmas at the hotel since his mother died, with staff going above and beyond to make sure he has a holiday to remember

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: Pat Gibson, current number one in the world quiz rankings, recalls the highs and lows of the game that made him a millionaire in 2004. Plus, a look at memorable moments from the games of celebrities Al Murray, Harry Hill, Paul O'Grady, Jo Brand and Nick Hancock

The programme features contributions from an array of the show's leading ladies - past and present - including Julie Hesmondhalgh, Sally Lindsay, Helen Worth, Alison King, Sue Nicholls, Sally Dynevor, Kate Ford, Dolly-Rose Campbell and Patti Clare, along with writers, producers and celebrity fans.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Robert, Alison, Amy and Paul pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Great British Escape
programme.desc: In the final episode, Paul heads down Kent's coastline to Botany Bay to discover more about tales of smuggling. Here, he meets locals Pete and George, who tell him how the soft, chalky white cliffs around Dover were perfect for smugglers to drill tunnels where they could hide their treasures. Later, Paul scales the great tower at the iconic Dover Castle, before making his way to Port Lympne Reserve, one of the UK's leading wildlife conservation centres

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: Road Trip
programme.desc: After last year's trip to the unlikely festive destination of Morocco, Gordon Ramsay is determined to celebrate Christmas properly this year, arranging a trip to the ultimate winter wonderland of Lapland. Gino D'Acampo has a letter he wants to hand deliver to Santa from his daughter Mia, Fred is keen to witness the once-in-a-lifetime wonder of the Northern Lights and Gordon is eager that they all encounter the culinary delights of the country. But along the way, they face plenty of distractions, including ice swimming, saunas, snowmobiles and snowballing

programme.desc: Documentary following life in the glamorous London hotel over the festive season, as staff prepare to welcome guests during the hotel's most profitable time of the year. Head butler Sean braces new recruit Michael for how busy he is going to be, while restaurant director Anne prepares the four restaurants and her 160 staff. Cameras also follow regular guest Howell Harris as he spends his first Christmas at the hotel since his mother died, with staff going above and beyond to make sure he has a holiday to remember

programme.title: Des O'Connor: The Ultimate Entertainer
programme.desc: A tribute to the longest-serving prime time TV host of all time, who topped theatre bills globally, including more than a thousand solo appearances at the London Palladium. And despite much mockery from Morecambe and Wise, he released 36 chart-topping albums. The programme tells how Des O'Connor went from Butlins Redcoat to become the ultimate entertainer, and features contributions from celebrity friends and admirers including Jonathan Ross, Jay Leno, Melanie Sykes, Bradley Walsh, Joe Pasquale, Alan Davies, Elaine Paige and Cliff Richard

However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes
